payload.answer: The new information conflicts with the existing claim that Sam works at Goldman. The safest durable update is to record an employment transition (left Goldman, joined Point72) and mark that without dates we cannot confirm which employer is currently active.
payload.decision: Store the new employment transition as a time-bounded/conditional update without overwriting the existing 'current employment' claim; mark that the current employer may have changed to Point72 after leaving Goldman.

entity_resolution.reason: Sam/Goldman/Point72 appear to match the same real-world entities implied by the existing fact, but no IDs or dates are provided.

payload.intent: Explain and resolve a conflict between an existing employment fact and a new employment transition fact for the same person.

memory_cards.statement: Potential contradiction: Existing memory says Sam works at Goldman, while new input says Sam left Goldman and joined Point72. The true current employer is unclear without dates.
Sam left Goldman and joined Point72 (employment transition per new input; current status depends on timing).

details: Added an employment_transition memory card reflecting the new claim without directly overwriting the existing 'works at Goldman' current-employment statement.
Logged a conflict_note indicating the current employer is ambiguous without timing information.

repair_options: Ask for the month/year (or exact date) when Sam left Goldman and started at Point72.
If the system tracks time, convert both statements into time-bounded facts (e.g., 'Sam worked at Goldman until <date>' and 'Sam joined Point72 on <date>').
If 'Sam works at Goldman' is believed to be outdated, replace it with the time-bounded Point72 fact and explicitly mark Goldman as former.
